See: <https://docs.deribit.com/v2/#private-cancel_block_rfq_quote> Rate limit: Matching engine endpoint (tier-based rate limiting) Scope: block_rfq:read_write # Arguments * `block_rfq_quote_id` - Optional ID of the Block RFQ quote * `label` - Optional user defined label for the Block RFQ quote (maximum 64 characters) * `block_rfq_id` - Optional ID of the Block RFQ # Returns Result containing the cancelled Block RFQ quote details

parameters.description: ID of the Block RFQ quote (optional)

deribit.name: get_address_book

See: <https://docs.deribit.com/v2/#private-get_address_book> Rate limit: 500 credits per request (non-matching engine) Scope: wallet:read # Arguments * `currency` - The currency symbol * `address_type` - Address book type (transfer, withdrawal, or deposit_source) # Returns Result containing array of address book entries

deribit.name: cancel_withdrawal

See: <https://docs.deribit.com/v2/#private-cancel_withdrawal> Rate limit: Non-matching engine endpoint Scope: wallet:read_write # Arguments * `currency` - The currency symbol (BTC, ETH, USDC, USDT, EURR) * `id` - The withdrawal id # Returns Cancel result with complete withdrawal information

deribit.name: cancel_all

parameters.name: detailed
parameters.description: When detailed is set to true output format is changed (optional)

parameters.name: freeze_quotes
parameters.description: Whether or not to reject incoming quotes for 1 second after cancelling (optional)

deribit.name: execute_block_trade
deribit.description: Execute block trade Creates block trade. The whole request have to be exact the same as in `private/verify_block_trade`, only role field should be set appropriately - it basically means that both sides have to agree on the same timestamp, nonce, trades fields and server will assure that role field is different between sides (each party accepted own role). binance.name: auto_cancel_all_open_orders
binance.description: Sets up auto-cancel for all open orders (TRADE) on Binance Coin-M Futures. See: <https://developers.binance.com/docs/derivatives/coin-margined-futures/trade/rest-api/Auto-Cancel-All-Open-Orders> POST /dapi/v1/countdownCancelAll Weight: 10 Requires API key and signature. This endpoint cancels all open orders of the specified symbol at the end of the specified countdown. This rest endpoint is designed to ensure your open orders are canceled in case of an outage. The endpoint should be called repeatedly as heartbeats so that the existing countdown time can be canceled and replaced by a new one. The system will check all countdowns approximately every 10 milliseconds, so please note that sufficient redundancy should be considered when using this function. We do not recommend setting the countdown time to be too precise or too small. Example usage: - Call this endpoint at 30s intervals with a countdownTime of 120000 (120s). - If this endpoint is not called within 120 seconds, all your orders of the specified symbol will be automatically canceled. - If this endpoint is called with a countdownTime of 0, the countdown timer will be stopped. # Arguments * `params` - The request parameters (see [`AutoCancelAllOpenOrdersRequest`]) # Returns An [`AutoCancelAllOpenOrdersResponse`] with the countdown configuration.

parameters.name: countdown_time
parameters.description: Countdown time in milliseconds (1000 for 1 second). Set to 0 to cancel the timer.

parameters.name: recv_window

binance.name: get_account_trades
binance.description: Fetches trades for a specific account and symbol or pair. See: <https://developers.binance.com/docs/derivatives/coin-margined-futures/trade/rest-api/Account-Trade-List> GET /dapi/v1/userTrades Weight: 20 with symbol, 40 with pair # Arguments * `params` - The request parameters (see [`AccountTradeListRequest`]) # Returns A vector of [`AccountTrade`] objects.

parameters.name: symbol
parameters.description: The trading symbol (e.g., "BTCUSD_PERP"). Either `symbol` or `pair` must be sent, but not both. Format: "{ASSET}USD_PERP" for perpetual contracts, "{ASSET}USD_{EXPIRY}" for delivery contracts.

parameters.name: pair
parameters.description: The trading pair (e.g., "BTCUSD"). Either `symbol` or `pair` must be sent, but not both. Format: "{ASSET}USD" where ASSET is the base asset (e.g., BTC, ETH).

parameters.name: order_id
parameters.description: The order ID to filter trades by. Can only be sent with `symbol`. Must be a valid order ID from a previous order.

parameters.name: start_time
parameters.description: Start time in milliseconds since epoch. Must be less than endTime if both are provided. Range: 0 to current timestamp.

parameters.name: end_time
parameters.description: End time in milliseconds since epoch. Must be greater than startTime if both are provided. Range: 0 to current timestamp.

parameters.name: from_id
parameters.description: Trade ID to fetch from. Default gets most recent trades. Cannot be sent with `pair`. Must be a valid trade ID from a previous trade.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of results to return. Default 50; max 1000. Range: 1 to 1000.

parameters.name: recv_window
parameters.description: The value cannot be greater than 60000. Range: 0 to 60000 milliseconds.

parameters.name: timestamp
parameters.description: Timestamp in milliseconds since epoch. Mandatory. Must be within 1000ms of server time.

Either quantity or price must be sent. # Arguments * `params` - The request parameters (see [`ModifyOrderRequest`]) # Returns A [`ModifyOrderResponse`] object with updated order details.

binance.name: get_position_adl_quantile
binance.description: Gets position ADL quantile estimation (USER_DATA) on Binance Coin-M Futures. See: <https://developers.binance.com/docs/derivatives/coin-margined-futures/trade/rest-api/Position-ADL-Quantile-Estimation> GET /dapi/v1/adlQuantile Weight: 5 Requires API key and signature. Query position ADL quantile estimation. - Values update every 30s. - Values 0, 1, 2, 3, 4 shows the queue position and possibility of ADL from low to high. - For positions of the symbol are in One-way Mode or isolated margined in Hedge Mode, "LONG", "SHORT", and "BOTH" will be returned to show the positions' adl quantiles of different position sides. - If the positions of the symbol are crossed margined in Hedge Mode: - "HEDGE" as a sign will be returned instead of "BOTH"; - A same value calculated on unrealized pnls on long and short sides' positions will be shown for "LONG" and "SHORT" when there are positions in both of long and short sides. # Arguments * `params` - The request parameters (see [`GetPositionAdlQuantileRequest`]) # Returns A [`GetPositionAdlQuantileResponse`] - array of position ADL quantile entries.

See: https://www.okx.com/docs-v5/en/#rest-api-public-data-get-option-market-data Rate limit: 20 requests per 2 seconds # Arguments * `request` - The option summary request parameters # Returns Response containing option market data

parameters.name: underlying
parameters.description: Underlying, only applicable to OPTION Either uly or instFamily is required. If both are passed, instFamily will be used.

parameters.name: inst_family
parameters.description: Instrument family, only applicable to OPTION Either uly or instFamily is required. If both are passed, instFamily will be used.

parameters.name: exp_time
parameters.description: Contract expiry date, the format is "YYMMDD", e.g. "200527"

okx.name: get_estimated_settlement_info
okx.description: Get estimated future settlement price Retrieve the estimated settlement price which will only have a return value one hour before the settlement. See: https://www.okx.com/docs-v5/en/#rest-api-public-data-get-estimated-settlement-price Rate limit: 10 requests per 2 seconds Rate limit rule: IP + Instrument ID # Arguments * `request` - The estimated settlement info request parameters # Returns Response containing the estimated settlement info

parameters.name: inst_id
parameters.description: Instrument ID, e.g. "XRP-USDT-250307", only applicable to FUTURES

okx.name: get_exchange_rate
okx.description: Get exchange rate This interface provides the average exchange rate data for 2 weeks See: https://www.okx.com/docs-v5/en/#rest-api-market-data-get-exchange-rate Rate limit: 1 request per 2 seconds # Returns Response containing the exchange rate information

okx.name: get_history_index_candles
okx.description: Get index candlesticks history Retrieve the candlestick charts of the index from recent years. See: https://www.okx.com/docs-v5/en/#rest-api-market-data-get-index-candlesticks-history Rate limit: 10 requests per 2 seconds # Arguments * `request` - The index candlesticks history request parameters # Returns Response containing the historical candlestick data

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: Pagination of data to return records earlier than the requested ts

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: Pagination of data to return records newer than the requested ts

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of results per request. Maximum is 100, default is 100

okx.name: get_index_candles
okx.description: Get index candlesticks Retrieve the candlestick charts of the index. This endpoint can retrieve the latest 1,440 data entries. Charts are returned in groups based on the requested bar. See: https://www.okx.com/docs-v5/en/#rest-api-market-data-get-index-candlesticks Rate limit: 20 requests per 2 seconds # Arguments * `request` - The index candlesticks request parameters # Returns Response containing the candlestick data

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: Pagination of data to return records earlier than the requested ts

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: Pagination of data to return records newer than the requested ts

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of results per request. Maximum is 100, default is 100

okx.name: get_mark_price
okx.description: Get mark price Retrieve mark price. We set the mark price based on the SPOT index and at a reasonable basis to prevent individual users from manipulating the market and causing the contract price to fluctuate. See: https://www.okx.com/docs-v5/en/#rest-api-public-data-get-mark-price Rate limit: 10 requests per 2 seconds Rate limit rule: IP + Instrument ID # Arguments * `request` - The mark price request parameters # Returns Response containing mark price data

parameters.name: inst_type
parameters.description: Instrument type. Required. MARGIN, SWAP, FUTURES, OPTION

parameters.name: inst_family
parameters.description: Instrument family. Optional. Applicable to FUTURES/SWAP/OPTION

okx.name: get_instruments
okx.description: Get available trading instruments Retrieve a list of instruments with open contracts. See: https://www.okx.com/docs-v5/en/#rest-api-public-data-get-instruments Rate limit: 20 requests per 2 seconds # Arguments * `request` - The instruments request parameters # Returns Response containing the list of available instruments

parameters.description: Currency for which to retrieve instruments.

bingx.name: get_historical_kline
bingx.description: Get historical K-line data Query historical K-line data for transaction prices. # Arguments * `request` - The historical K-line request parameters # Returns Response containing historical K-line data array # Rate Limit - IP: 100 requests per 10 seconds (Group 1) # API Documentation - Endpoint: GET /openApi/market/his/v1/kline - Content-Type: request body(application/json) # Time Rules - If startTime and endTime are not sent, the latest K-line data is returned by default - If startTime and endTime are sent, the latest K-line data up to endTime is returned by default - If startTime is sent but endTime is not sent, the latest K-line data starting from startTime is returned by default - If startTime is not sent but endTime is sent, the latest K-line data up to endTime is returned by default

parameters.name: symbol
parameters.description: Trading pair, e.g., BTC-USDT, please use uppercase letters (required)

parameters.name: interval
parameters.description: Time interval, reference field description (required) Valid values: 1m, 3m, 5m, 15m, 30m, 1h, 2h, 4h, 6h, 8h, 12h, 1d, 3d, 1w, 1M

parameters.name: symbol

bitget.name: get_account_assets
bitget.description: Get account assets for spot trading Retrieves balance information for the authenticated account. Can filter by specific coin or asset type. # Arguments * `request` - The request parameters specifying filters # Rate Limit 10 requests per second per UID # Returns A result containing the account assets or an error

parameters.name: coin
parameters.description: Token name, e.g. USDT. Used for querying positions of a single coin.

parameters.name: asset_type
parameters.description: Asset type filter - `hold_only`: Position coin (assets that have holdings) - `all`: All coins including zero balances Default is `hold_only`

bitget.name: get_order_history
bitget.description: Get historical orders Retrieves order history for the authenticated account with optional filters. Only supports data within 90 days. Older data can be downloaded from the web. # Arguments * `request` - The request parameters with optional filters # Rate Limit 20 requests per second per UID # Returns A result containing the order history or an error

parameters.name: start_time
parameters.description: The record start time for the query (Unix milliseconds)

parameters.name: end_time
parameters.description: The end time of the record for the query (Unix milliseconds) The interval between startTime and endTime must not exceed 90 days

parameters.name: id_less_than
parameters.description: Requests content on the page before this ID (older data)

bitget.name: cancel_order
bitget.description: Cancel a specific spot trading order Cancels an existing order by order ID or client order ID. # Arguments * `request` - The cancel order request parameters # Rate Limit 10 requests per second per UID # Returns A result containing the cancel order response or an error

parameters.name: new_client_order_id
parameters.description: New client order ID for the cancel

bitget.name: get_fills
bitget.description: Get trade fills Retrieves trade execution details for the authenticated account. Only supports data within 90 days. Older data can be downloaded from the web. # Arguments * `request` - The request parameters with optional filters # Rate Limit 10 requests per second per UID # Returns A result containing the trade fills or an error

parameters.name: start_time
parameters.description: The start time of the orders (Unix milliseconds) For Managed Sub-Account, startTime cannot be earlier than binding time

parameters.name: end_time
parameters.description: The end time of fulfilled orders (Unix milliseconds) The interval between startTime and endTime must not exceed 90 days

parameters.name: id_less_than
parameters.description: Requests content on the page before this ID (older data) Value should be the tradeId from the corresponding interface

bitget.name: place_order
bitget.description: Place a spot trading order Places a new order for spot trading with the specified parameters. # Arguments * `request` - The order placement request parameters # Rate Limit 10 requests per second per UID 1 request per second per UID for copy trading traders # Returns A result containing the order placement response or an error

parameters.name: force
parameters.description: Execution strategy (invalid when orderType is market)

parameters.name: price
parameters.description: Limit price (required for limit orders) The decimal places of price can be obtained from Get Symbol Info

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: Order amount For Limit and Market-Sell orders: represents the number of base coins For Market-Buy orders: represents the number of quote coins

parameters.name: receive_window
parameters.description: Valid time window (Unix milliseconds) If set, request is valid only when server time is within receiveWindow

bitget.name: get_current_orders
bitget.description: Get current unfilled orders Retrieves all unfilled orders for the authenticated account with optional filters. # Arguments * `request` - The request parameters with optional filters # Rate Limit 20 requests per second per UID # Returns A result containing the current orders or an error

parameters.name: start_time
parameters.description: The record start time for the query (Unix milliseconds)

parameters.name: end_time
parameters.description: The end time of the record for the query (Unix milliseconds)

parameters.name: id_less_than
parameters.description: Requests content on the page before this ID (older data)

bitget.name: get_order_info
bitget.description: Get detailed information about a specific order Retrieves comprehensive order information including fill status, fees, and timestamps. # Arguments * `request` - The order info request (by order ID or client order ID) # Rate Limit 20 requests per second per UID # Returns A result containing the order information or an error

parameters.name: order_id
parameters.description: Order ID (either orderId or clientOrderId is required)

parameters.name: client_order_id
parameters.description: Client order ID (either orderId or clientOrderId is required)
